reviews.review_text:
Also, das was ich vom Campingplatz gesehen habe hat mir sehr gut gefallen, also wenn ich noch mal in der gegend bin würde ich dort gerne Urlaub machen.
Vom wohnwagen ins Wasser,
Schöne abendsonne genißen, platz fürs auto gibt es auch.
Einfach schön

Wonderful park. It's been here since the 1960s! Great location on the ocean and inlet. Beautifully maintained, clean ammenities. Cotton Tree Village (across the road) is very cute (cafes, shops, etc). And the Boathouse restaurant on the beach is right next door. It is a nice get-away park.
Perfect spot, close to shops and amenities but still feels like a secluded beach side spot. Facilites are spotless and well looked after. We will be back.

So disappionting. I would never book here again in advance. There is no consideration on mgmt side to move few dates even if conditions are unlivable and unbearable due to weather. Congratulations on making few unfair hundreds!
Enjoy Cotton Tree each year. Great location on the sunny coast.
Nice place. Right on the water. Clean facilities. Walking distance to cafes and FoodWorks, or a short drive to Woolworths.
Awesome spot awesome people.Great staff.

The van park is a lovely spot but there is a terrible and dangerous problem with Junkies and the element entering the grounds from the beach front robbing and pillaging as well as banging on vans and trying to enter my sister's tent. Management need to step up and hire roaming security as it's not safe at present especially outside peak holiday times when park has lower occupancy. Otherwise again the park itself is a nice spot.
Easy experience with everything at the front door. Wear the kids out swimming all day and then walk up the road and a multitude of options for dining experiences from easy to more formal settings!
Amazing location right by the inlet. Easy swim for the kids, calm waters and great ameneties. Kitchen, showers, toulets and plenty of bins. Very clean location

Had booked a week in my son's pop up van. We are seniors. Became unwell unable to travel. Àsked for a change of date. Not obliging. Wanted $30 for this and full payment to change date. Felt the park could have done a little more. Have stayed there previously.

We arrived on Friday and was was told by another camper during week days when the park was quiet they turn the water down to save money, so we'll see. Paying $76 for a week day and $84 for weekends is very expensive to say the least with no pool or other entertainment, which other parks seem to have which are cheaper. Unfortunately everywhere is book solid with sporting activities happening this weekend. What happened to the affordable holidays for families, if you're travellers, free camping is a must otherwise you might as well rent, its just as expensive or get a hotel. Caravan parks seem to think its there god given right to charge the earth for a 3x5 patch of dirt and a bit of water and electricity which wouldn't cost more then $10 a day. You say you match the other caravan parks but you don't say how many other caravan parks you own!
Great park
Location, location, location...... A beautiful place weather you have kids or not, and everything is close and handy.
We loved our stay as usual. This is simply the best located place to stay if you are wanting a relaxed camping style holiday.
Obwohl noch zahlreiche Plätze frei waren, wurde uns der mieseste Platz neben einem Müllhaufen zugewiesen, was auch auf Nachfrage nicht geändert wurde. Toilette und Waschräume waren auch mäßig sauber
It might help if you had the right number on the site so families can book or at least reply the emails that were send 3 weeks and a week ago about bookings

Loved our stay at Cotton Tree Caravan Park. This was the perfect location for us. Handy to everything we needed. Beach, shops, restaurants,  coffee shops and places to walk of a morning. No need to drive the car during our week stay.|DEFINITELY RETURNING!!!

Due to illness had to cancel my visit to the Park. Tried to call 2 days prior to my arrival, but after being told I was ‘2nd in the cue’ to be answered I was told to ‘leave a message’ which I did, no return call has been forthcoming. I also sent 2 emails, one direct to their email and one via the booking page contact form. In the email I included a Doctors Certificate to support my case for using my booking in a future visit….that was 10 days ago, and no response has been received. I have tried to call the office as well over the last 3 days, each time waiting for 40min+ each time only to be told to ‘leave a message’, which I did, no contact from them has been made.
I am very puzzled  by the lack of ability to contact this Park, and also their inability to respond to emails, I guess they don’t need my patronage, so I think Fingal will be my campground to visit going forward.

If I could give 0 star rating I would.
We had booked to go (our first visit) 13 Feb however Wed 11/2 morning, extreme weather warnings for SEQ were forecast for the weekend  from Thursday night onward.   I called Wed 11/2 to try and change dates.  The first phone call attempt I was on hold for over an hour then was cut off, only on the 4th attempt did someone eventually answer the phone.  Explaining my concern of the forecasted extreme weather situation, I asked to change the dates.  I was told without hesitation that cancellation policy had to be done 7 days prior to stay.  I advised the person that I was not cancelling the booking (paid for in full)  I just wanted to change the dates as the warnings were for extreme storms, flash flooding and strong winds and we would prefer not to be in a tent during this event! There was absolutely no understanding and was told no change of date was possible. Furthermore if we did go that it would be our decision and the that the Holiday Park would accept no responsibility for damage or injury.  Obviously we did not want to be in a tent during extreme weather conditions so we did not go.  Needless to say, we never got to go, but will certainly never be visiting.
Great location on the beach. Lots of vacancies in the middle of the week.

Cotton Tree tourist park the place to soak up the Sunshine Coast! Big park, easy access to river and sea. Plenty of shops or clubs to visit without driving. Amenities all up to date and clean, just a shame it's so far from home!!😁

We booked on line after seeing a promotion for pay for 3 days and stay for 4. This suited us we were here for work for 4 days with our motor home . I went ahead and booked …. What I thought  was the deal. 2 days into our stay we received a txt to advise of the check out time the next day . My husband went to the office to discuss this as we still had our last free day to stay.
Staff said that it looks like we missed a step when booking and didn’t add the promotion code. She said that she would ask the manager.

Later I had a call from the same staff member advising that the promotion had closed and that we would have to pay for the additional day !!

I asked to speak to the manager, at which she said that she could give me an email address to send my question. I sent the email advising of my situation, thinking that common sense and old fashioned customer service would prevail.

Crickets …. So I popped on after work & spoke to another young lady which said the same thing , with no empathy or compassion for our situation. She said that we’d have to pay $71 for the extra night if we wanted to stay.
I asked if she could offer us a discount considering what has happened, the answer was no , again no empathy at all.

It’s ridiculous that these big chains can carry on like this, sucking us into their online deals and not being accountable when the processes are not simple for the customer. There is absolutely no room for error , we are all human and it’s bound to happen.

The park itself is a great spot , but thats all that is on offer here. I won’t be back.

Cotton Tree Beach is awesome! We always have fun there and I was lucky to get a booking, the place is always fully booked and no wonder. Right next to the river and the beach. Restaurants are close and it's a small walk from town. The amenities are clean and so are the camp sites. The staff is always friendly and helpful. You must visit this camping spot! We had a powered tent site.

Great location but over the years and change of management it seems staff are less interested in talking to you and push you toward using the app.  Cabins lack simple wine glasses and crockery and supply of toilet paper, detergent and body wash is stingey
Lots of showers and toilets.   Very clean.   In a prime location

A great family place to stay for a holiday. Very relaxing with the beach and river access right there. Easy access to surrounding Activites, shops etc. lots of people, but nice and quiet at night times.

We have been holidaying at Cotton Tree for the past 16 years|we were very surprised how many great improvements the new managers  |have made to improve the safety of the park eg new fencing completely enclosing the park including gates at entrances. in the past a lot of non campers wandering around the park and items were going missing and the safety of the children riding there bike is great the amenities are amazingly very  clean the staff are always very pleasant and helpful , the grounds staff do an amazing job in the heat trimming any  grass which is growing fast around the camp site

Perfect location, well maintained holiday park and friendly check-in staff.
Have been coming for nearly 20yr and it’s never disappointed.

Next to the beach surf club walking distance
Plus shops over the road.

Very clean, well maintained park, However, security for amenities block 4 was never fixed.||Also, the fact that residents cannot use the laundromat with cash and hence a considerable amount of angst was experienced by some.||I also missed the coffee van which added to the atmosphere.||Just wondering about Christmas lighting in all the wonderful cotton trees would add to the Christmas vibe.||Yours sincerely,|June Kirk

Great location only a few facts the public access is to free with people walking through the park to & from the beach |The grounds staff mowing the lawn around the vans with small stone wood chips etc flying from the mower was hit just below the eye|Other than that not bad
Haben auf diesem Camping 2 Nächte gestanden. Kann nichts negatives berichten. Deshalb bekommt er aufgrund seiner Lage und seiner sauberen Sanitäranlagen 5 Sterne.

We love cotton tree and the park is looking the best I've seen it! Great camp kitchen, including a big tele to watch the cricket. And lovely amenities. The sites are a great size too, you don't feel like you're squished in. We will be back soon!
This is a really nice spot on the river and the park facilities are very clean.|The beach and water are amazing but keep an eye on your kids if its a big run out tide.
One of my Favourite places ❤️ 😍

I want to say; Don’t go here!  Because the less you all go and stay the more sites available for us to choose from. It’s probably our favorite park to stay at.  Perfect location.  You have everything at your fingertips.

There are lots of different sites and most are hit or miss on what youll get. Some sites need fixing up others have had new slab and power outlet.  Some are just old  & junky looking.  (I So wish parks allowed you to choose a specific site online during booking process).

Some waterfront sites are better than others but most are crammed in with limited space.  The tent waterfront sites are tops.   Except if there’s rain wind etc.  then you should book somewhere else
Went Camping on the weekend during storm it didn’t stop us! The place was very clean and location was the best! We will go back again definitely!!

After over 15 years of loyal patronage and creating family memories at this park, the way this situation was handled is disheartening. While I understand the need for policies, my specific complaint concerns the rigid enforcement of rules without any consideration for context or tenure.
There was absolutely no middle ground offered. Instead of working with long-term guests to find a reasonable solution or compromise, the decision was binary and dismissive. Seems to be an ongoing problem based on articles and other similar complaints.

Stayed at the holiday park in our caravan during October long weekend was very busy the amenities were clean. With the river at one end and the beach at the other and the weather was great that is where everyone was down on the water. We went down and swam and played in the sun for a couple of hours great to relax. Next time we will book earlier and ask for a water front view.
Across the road a few cafes, shops and a butchers all walking distance. Had dinner at the club near by nice club and great food.
Great spot. Sadly got my good joggers stolen. More security cameras pls.
Very clean and recently renovated nice quite place for night stop over

Great beach location. As per other reviews, crowded, not enough rubbish bins, challenges with staff - and very expensive.|And they also take money from your card without your consent. |We were 4 mothers with 5 kids (ranging from 4 months to 3 years). We requested sites together, so we could help each other - they messed this up. We got moved onto site 40 which was situated under a large tree which hosted many bush turkeys - which pooped all over EVERYTHING.|On check-out day, we spent hours trying to get initial poo off the site/our gear/the caravan/car - it was almost impossible to see through the windscreen to drive, it was so bad. The kind neighbour campers were helping watch the 2 year olds and loan us a hose and brush so we thought we could try to clean and hose everything (including the actual camp site). We spent significant time cleaning to leave it all in a presentable condition. Our actions were in good faith - and the delay was caused by circumstances beyond our control - wildlife fouling.|Yes we did lose track of time, scraping poop off and then went to rinse after. Despite our clear efforts to leave the site and our gear clean - and the fact that the car and caravan could have been moved at any time had they needed the site. We were approached by staff and spoken to in an aggressive manner. We complied and moved immediately. At the park exit - at the boom gate, they refused to let us leave and we were told a $30 fee had been charged to my card without authority.|This action is not legally permissible under Australian Consumer Law - third parties are not entitled to make deductions from a consumers credit card without explicit consent.|We were never advised of a possible fee and on searching their website - "Cotton Tree Caravan Park does not advertise a specific late check-out fee on its official or associated pages."|Rude staff, awful communication. Expensive camping trip to start with, then they just take money out of your account - without your consent. I would not recommend staying there.

Great beach location. As per other reviews,  crowded, not enough rubbish bins, challenges with staff - and very expensive.

And they also take money from your card without your consent.

We were 4 mothers with 5 kids (ranging from 4 months to 3 years). We requested sites together, so we could help each other - they messed this up. We got moved onto site 40 which was situated under a large tree which hosted many bush turkeys - which pooped all over EVERYTHING.

On check-out day, we spent hours trying to get initial poo off the site/our gear/the caravan/car - it was almost impossible to see through the windscreen to drive, it was so bad. The kind neighbour campers were helping watch the 2 year olds and loan us a hose and brush so we thought we could try to clean and hose everything (including the actual camp site). We spent significant time cleaning to leave it all in a presentable condition. Our actions were in good faith - and the delay was caused by circumstances beyond our control - wildlife fouling.

Yes we did lose track of time, scraping poop off and then went to rinse after. Despite our clear efforts to leave the site and our gear clean - and the fact that the car and caravan could have been moved at any time had they needed the site. We were approached by staff and spoken to in an aggressive manner. We complied and moved immediately. At the park exit - at the boom gate, they refused to let us leave and we were told a $30 fee had been charged to my card without authority.

This action is not legally permissible under Australian Consumer Law - third parties are not entitled to make deductions from a consumers credit card without explicit consent.

We were never advised of a possible fee and on searching their website - "Cotton Tree Caravan Park does not advertise a specific late check-out fee on its official or associated pages."

Rude staff, awful communication. Expensive camping trip to start with, then they just take money out of your account - without your consent. I would not recommend staying there.

Absolutely love our time there, the location is perfect for little humans, amenities are great etc.

Left a bit sour after we were charged extra for leaving a little later due to having to rudimentary wash our set up (our campsite was under a tree which hosted many many bush turkeys overnight (100+ poop to clean).

Would recommend the host not renting that site (40) to the public while the turkey are residing there overnight. Or at least give a warning.

We camped close to the beach and it was awesome to hear the waves all night. Great park, very clean, no mosquitoes or sandflies where we camped in the tent sites. The amenities were always clean, great hot showers with plenty of water pressure. We went during the week and it was great, fairly quiet and lots of room. Will definitely come back again, we had a great time.
We live on the Gold Coast but staying here is a world away. Will be back.
Powered site at a favourite location when travelling.
Visited with a motorhome on a powered slab site.  It is a nice campsite with plenty of rv/tent sites, cabins and amenities, which are taken well care of. Located next to the ocean and aquatic centre

Pos:Beautiful spot on Cotton Tree beach, cyrstal clear calm waterways great for kids!
Views and sunsets are one of the best on this coastline.
Unfortunatley was $80 for an out of season non powerd stand! And after taking the money they never left a late checkin entry, so could not get in after 5pm.

I didnt end up going there, so the quality of rooms and service are still mysterious to me😆, however, the position is the IDEAL Sunny Coast spot.
I have actually been to the surf club not that long ago, just down the road, and it had great food, great view and was a bustling atmosphere😃🤙
Location you can't beat
Excellent location, clean amenities, plenty of kitchen space bathrooms etc and staff are friendly and helpful.

Far too expensive.
Check in is worst than coming through Customs.

Just spent another amazing 8 weeks at this amazing park in our van and lived every minute. Office staff couldnt be more helpful and always professional  and polite and also like to mention the ground staff especially  Reece and Brian who always had time for a hello and sometimes a quick chat.  We have been coming to this park for many many years and will continue to do so.   Thank you cotton tree.
Clean amenities, beach in one side, river on the other,  what can be wrong!

We stayed at Cotton Tree Holiday Park on a powered site near the waterfront, and it was the perfect spot for us. We arrived just in time for sunset and couldn’t have asked for a better view.

The whole park felt peaceful and not overcrowded, which made it even more enjoyable. Every facility was spotless — bathrooms, kitchens, and shared areas were all exceptionally clean and well-kept. I absolutely loved the PIN access for the bathrooms; it made me feel safe and secure, especially when heading there at night.

Having direct access to the waterfront was a real treat, and we enjoyed relaxing in the sand just steps from our site. The park is also in an ideal location with restaurants, cafés, and the town all within close reach.

Highly recommend Cotton Tree Holiday Park for anyone looking for a clean, secure, and perfectly located stay by the water.
Camped in my own van, good site and amenities. Very busy, therefore amenities congested. Convenient for beach and cafes. Staff polite and efficient but one night rate was expensive in my view.

Top Platz für ein Wohnmobile.
Strand (in zwei Richtungen) von jeden Platz schnell erreichbar (1-4 Minuten).
Tolle Ausstatung und viele Badhäuser und Kochinselnen. Sehr sauber!!!
Rezeption ist sehr hilfsbereit und machen das Möglicherweise.
Wir haben zwei Tage dran gehängt. Toller Surfstrand direkt vor der Tür mit zwei Richtungen zur Auswahl - wie die Welle/Wind kommt.

$80 dollars a night for a site is equal to $560 a week. For a tiny space. The location is amazing, but the facilities and service do not reflect the price at all...

Also, as if $80 were not enough, they charge you extra for late check-ins ...
Great location, very expensive for power, water and a parking spot but very clean and close to everything you need.
I honestly don’t know how they can maintain their business. Management of these park makes it undesirable to support them. It’s a shame they have such key locations

Very large park, caravans packed in. Way to busy. The camp kitchens are appalling, the worst we have encountered in 3 months around Australia. Only one kitchen  is  enclosed, the rest are open concept. Too bad if its freezing cold or boiling hot. , 2 electric bbq are supplied, no oven, no hot plates to cook vegetables or pan fried meals. Noc ooking implements, 1 pot, 1 toaster 1 jug, cheap $7 kmart  and to add insult, a bread maker!!!.
Amenity block, straight out of a penetenturary. No hot water for hand washing, the dryer blows cold air. The appalling mop to use after your shower, please spend some money and buy something useful.
This park is so overrated, apart from staff there is not much going for it. Also don't think you can swim here, its the mouth of the river so not permitted. NO where  does it say in there brochures or on internet that you can't swim, very misleading information. Lastly very overpriced, find somewhere else.!!!!!! $80 a night is outrageous.

This is not a place to holiday at. It felt like a cult of unhappiness, lead by the old lady with glasses who features in the social media posts for all the Sunshine Coast caravan parks trying to entice us to come stay. But there is nothing fun here and nobody nice either. What a disappointing experience, for a genuinely beautiful location. Something is really wrong here.

This park is one of our favourites. The reception staff could have been a bit more helpful abt an easy enquiry.
The only real complaint I have is why are the amenities cleaned at 8am every morning. Most people are getting up (winter) to find the cleaners are working away. You're then told to use the other amenities, (which is fine) to find all the toilets are being used! Very busy on the weekends. A lot of complaints were heard abt this. We've travelled extensively around Oz, and in most cases amenities are cleaned a bit later in the morning.
However, the park is very neat and tidy and the amenities and camp kitchen spotless.
Very tight sites

We stayed in our caravan on a powered site, the sites were so tiny and the streets narrow, we had to get people to move their cars from sites to try and squash our van in, not only us but nearly all had the same complaint, they really need a tractor service, the sites were small and uneventful, mostly older retired people, walking distance to shops, clubs and cafes and nice beach

20 minutes wait on the phone  , u are No.1 in the queue don't hang up,
Lady answered the call
' o we were on a break '
Charming...

Can't really tell you much about this place as we decided not to stay. But we did look around and what we did see is this.   The location is beautiful of course as it is right on the beach.  The office staff very helpful and friendly. The park was fully booked except for one slab we saw that was right alongside the entrance driveway, so not great. More of a car park spot than a holiday spot. In fact most of the camping spots we saw were really small and everyone packed really tightly in side by side with no real privacy. I reckon you could easily hear your neighbours snore at night. We have been to lots of caravan parks, and this is one we will probably never choose because $80 a night is way too much for a plot size that would be the absolute minimum. Even for the very experienced, fitting a caravan into allocated spot would be almost impossible if the others were full around you. Did not check the shower blocks and laundry eymtc but no reason to think they would be bad. The park clean and tidy and well kept.
Great stay overnight recommend

Amazing location and great park facilities. The only only negative I have would be - stock better toilet paper 🤣

--- edit 2 years later ---
I've dropped my rating from 5 stars to 3stars - stayed here a bunch of times now and the price goes up every time. Was meant to stay in unpowered camping this weekend but had to cancel on the day due to covid. Management refused to carry forward as a credit so $160 lost for nothing. That's their prerogative and fits to their terms and conditions, but I can't rate 5 stars with that sort of below average customer service - if it was peak season I would understand, but guaranteed they didn't need to turn away unpowered campers for our booking. Beyond that the park offers clean bathrooms and a small kitchenette but that's it - no pool, no other useful offering. Definitely not worth the money anymore....
Greta, fun, lots of things to do
One of the best campgrounds I’ve been to — clean bathrooms, friendly staff, and beautiful views.

My husband and I stayed at this cotton tree holiday park the staff absolutely rude they required payment upfront which I paid for two nights. Accommodation stayed one night. I left the next morning and I made numerous phone calls to speak to management. No one has called me yet. I would not repeat. I would NOT recommend Cotton-tree holiday park to  anybody the Internet is appalling even though they say free Wi-Fi and I will never ever stay there EVER AGAIN and I highly,highly,highly recommend it lngenia holiday park they are absolutely phenomenal.

Beautiful location, premium price..
Booked selected sites together in advance but were changed without notification, consideration or apology.
(sites we booked are unaffected and vacant)
Ladies amenities are less than clean, shower water temperatures reduced, lots of complaints for cold showers.
Average management, shame for such a potentially beautiful park.

Just beautiful!
Heaps of spaces available, and tidy amenities.

One of my absolute faves! 🌟 Cotton Tree Holiday Park ticks all the boxes.

Staff are super friendly and welcoming – huge thanks to the whole team! 🙌😊
Amenities are large, spotless, and well-maintained 🚿✨
The park itself is tidy and cared for, making it a really pleasant place to stay 🌿👍
Laundry on site, clotheslines to dry your gear, heaps of bins everywhere, plus a handy dump point ♻️👕🗑️

And the best bit? It’s right by the beach 🏖️🌊 and just minutes from everything Cotton Tree town has to offer – cafes, shops, markets, you name it!

Can’t wait to come back. 1000% recommending this spot! 🏕️❤️
Peaceful oasis on the Sunshine Coast.

If I could I would give no stars .Staff are so unfriendly. If Bri smiled her face would crack. You have to pay a week upfront,
fair enough but when you get there another week is required. When questioning Belinda why it was 2 weeks upfront she said it wasn't and went into this almighty rant. When I went to school 1 plus 1 equalled 2. Don't ever ask for help because staff don't give a damn. It's all about the money. And watch them with the loyalty card because they say they discount but they don't and when you query this and ask them to show where the discount is they cave. Oh but then they say they will refund but don't unless you keep at them and then you are seen as being a nuisance . We will never stay at their parks again. And they have 5 parks on the Sunshine coast which is a real shame. Sunshine coast council need to look at these reviews and get new managers .

A Beautiful Spot, But Disappointing Service

Cotton Tree has always been one of our favourite places to stay. The location is absolutely stunning, and we've made so many great memories here over the years. Unfortunately, since the change in ownership, we've experienced a significant decline in communication and customer service, which has been confusing, frustrating and at times, quite rude.

I don’t normally leave negative reviews, as I understand how impactful they can be. But I truly hope this feedback encourages the current owners and staff to improve their communication and organisation. Cotton Tree is a special place and I’d love nothing more than to enjoy our holidays there again without the stress.

We had planned, as we do every year, to camp at Cotton Tree during the September school holidays. However yesterday we received an email notifying us that our booking had been cancelled and refunded due to works being carried out in that area of the grounds. What’s more disappointing is that we were told not to call, as there would be no opportunity to secure an alternative spot.

While I understand that unforeseen circumstances can arise, the lack of notice and unwillingness to assist us, especially given the issues with communication over the past couple of years, makes this particularly disheartening. It now leaves our family and friends scrambling to find a suitable camping spot during one of the busiest times of the year.

I genuinely hope Cotton Tree can turn things around. It’s such a beautiful destination and it would be a real shame to see it lose the community’s trust due to poor service.
We had a great holiday here

Although a great location of a campsite, close to the beach and a 20 min walk to sunshine plaza along the water, my interactions with staff through the campsites polices left a sour taste. Check-in in strictly from 11. Arriving at 10am I was told to come back in an hour, even though the campsite was like half empty. There was no compromise.
The text messages received have strong language that I didn't find inviting, especially with the use of all caps.
A sign for the bathrooms say cleaning is "from" 9am, so at 8.30 the toilets were closed and I had to walk across the park to the other block.
Another staff member during the day going around taking photos of campsites, which I've never experienced at any other park, which I just found odd.

Stayed at cotton tree CP and the park looks great. The staff were very welcoming and check in was easy. The amenities were very clean and smelled great too. Grounds were kept really nice. The staff we seen out in the park were very friendly and greeted us with smiles. Would recommend staying here.

Very disappointed with the customer service, especially from Matilda.

I had a really disappointing experience here, mainly due to the lack of support and poor customer service from Matilda. When I arrived after hours, the instructions were confusing and there was no staff available to help.

They never answered the phone when I needed help. When I asked for a code to use the shower for my baby (which clearly had a baby symbol on the sign), I was told it was only for people with disabilities. After insisting, I was finally given the code, but it was done in a rude and unhelpful manner.

I expected better communication and care for guests-unfortunately, that was not my experience. I would not recommend staying here unless the customer service improves significantly

Unfortunately not a pleasant experience this time around
Toilets and showers were dirty
Soap dispensers empty even after cleaning crew were present
Kitchen wasn’t opened on time

This park is fantastic.
Nice clean amenities.
Close to shops, cafes and the water.
So relaxing here.

We've come to cotton tree a number of times and unfortunately this year has by far been the worst.

The staff at check in were very friendly however aside from checking in, this was the only time we saw staff other than the security guards and cleaners.

The amenities were gross. Urinals blocked, thanks to unsupervised feral children, were not cleaned out for days. Rubbish throughout the amenities and constantly running out of toilet paper due to the infrequent cleans during peak holiday time.

The main camp kitchen/ pavilion was completely overrun every single day, again by unsupervised feral children, which was never dealt with aside from a blanket sms threat and the security guard closing it at 10pm.

The scooters and bikes were out of control this year. Kids coming out from behind parked cars, not looking, not wearing helmets and going way too fast for safety.

The dump points were not emptied during one of the busiest times of the year. All dump points were almost full before the weekend was even getting started.

Lots of rules on check in, about distances from boundaries and cars having to be parked on sites, however this was never policed and made it hard to manoeuvre with and without the van on the back.

More and more randoms coming and going through the park at all hours of the day and night - many of these people flying through on ebikes and scooters with no care for safety.

The place felt really out of control this visit. These issues can't be resolved with a few passive aggressive blanket text messages to guests, you need to be actively monitoring and taking action when people are blatantly disrespecting the rules. Considering what we're now paying to stay here, it's your obligation to provide us with these simple measures in return.
Great place to unwind highly recommended

Cottontree CP, is one of the most overrated CP we have stayed in over the 3 years  of travelling full- time & the most expensive nightly rate also . We would never stay  or recommend  Cotton Tree CP, it was such a huge disappointment. 1/ The sites are really small & close together & completely jammed packed in. 2/Our site was quite uneven even though it had a slab this is just ridiculous for any caravan park. We chocked one side up but you shouldn’t have too and it made other vans have to wait longer for us to set up that came in behind us.   3/ The park is not well maintained period it looks so scrappy and at the time we stayed not secured so anyone could walk into the park which was happening nightly, lock up your valuables  !!  a)Both dump points were completely  full for a good few days and the smell & waste ( shite)  was over flowing & was beyond disgusting & is actually a health issue  b)Blocked grates, so when it rained the street were full of water. Also the grates clank continually making  quite a loud noise every time a car or child went over them on bikes etc. c) Mens urinal blocked in middle block. d) Hand Dryers not operational & useless when they are working, so people use the toilet paper to dry their hands which means the toilets were constantly running out of toilet paper, just Invest in paper towel for hand drying !! 4/ CP Policy states one vehicle  per site however due to the size of the gazebos, tents,  vans, swags , boats and extras people are putting onto their sites, cars were always parked halfway over the road making it very difficult to reverse or get in or off site with a van. The site behind us actually had a car and a boat the later was so close to our  our van we couldn’t walk around the back. 5/ The biggest concern is with the cars park half way on the road, children were darting out behind them & even going 8km in a car, there will be a a serious injury or fatality here because parents weirdly think the children are safe in a Caravan Park , we didn’t see any supervision not once !! Children flying around the park on bikes & scooter some motorised day & night   6/ The caravan park is not managed well & it seems once they have your money they turn a blind eye to what is happening in their park. However they will be liable when a tragic event occurs for not enforcing their own policies  !!  7/ Due diligence is required around the park & continual text msg reminders are needed daily so patrons understand the parks management are serious with their policies & msg also helps remind patrons to adhere to the rules, so that everybody can enjoy their time. 8/ Amenities are looking old & always dirty mostly due to the lack of respect from other patrons but they need to be maintained twice daily when the caravan park is at a full capacity as it was when we stayed. 9/ Noise restrictions need to be adhered to, on our week stay, daily small children on scooters & bikes were screaming from 5.45am till well past 11.00pm with little to mostly no supervision this is beyond in exceptable.😜10/ Children harassing the Bush Turkeys while their parents laughed & egged them on to chase them is plain cruel & needs to be addressed !! This was a daily occurrence.
11/ The bathroom doors are so loud when opening and closing you wouldn’t want to have a site near them just saying !! 12/ Camp kitchens are very basic.  13/ One of the most unfriendly parks we have stayed , both management & patrons  13/ The Beach area has been washed away although obviously not the parks fault it’s certainly not as picturesque as it once was and was so much better 10+ years ago

Disappointing!! Had a booking for 4 nights, unfortunately on our day of arrival we broke down and couldn’t make our booking. We phoned to inform and was told we would only get a refund if the site was resold (completely fair enough) and that we could rebook if we wanted to when we had our car fixed. We phoned a couple of days later once the car was fixed to arrange moving our booking, only for 2 nights instead of 4 and was more than happy to lose out on the additional two nights we’d paid for. We were told moving our booking would not be an issue and to let them know once’s we knew when we’d like to change the dates to. On phoning back to arrange this the realty was very different to what we were told. Waited two hours for someone to call back to assist but that didn’t happen and they refused to answer our calls. We tried the next morning to be told their terms and conditions prevent us from moving our booking and due to the cancelled booking there is nothing we can do. Complete contrasting information to what was initially said. Very disappointing! Given the time of year and campsite being half full you’d think some allowances could be made during unforeseen stressful circumstances!
A great spot for families. Pity it rained our whole holiday 😑
Excellent position, its the North Coast version of Tallebudgera, lots to offer.
I love the location of the park.
We love coming to this Amazing caravan park, and the sunsets are a bonus!

Great location, super helpful office staff. Plenty of amenities. Cabin was well stocked with all linen and utensils and air-conditioning. Fans in bedroom and lounge room.  Verandah with table and chairs. Couldn't ask for more considering we arrived the day the all clear was given after ex cyclone Alfie.

With the 35% price rise & the tales told about the reasons it clearly spells out robbery/cash grab,the excuse is that everything went up off COVID & we need to maintain our amenities when one amenity was removed & COVID was years ago there still using that as an excuse... unbelievable, council capped the increases to  a max of 20% and yet this still gets approved...what's going on.
Very sad that they take the population for a ride

Nice spot on the Sunshine Coast. Close to coffee shops, eateries, and the surf club.
Shady places for camping, and to park a campervan. Easy and close access to the beach and river.
Amenities are clean, and grounds well maintained. The permanent accommodation rooms look modern and comfortable.
I am staying in a motor home this visit but will consider a room next visit... I will be back!
Beautiful location. Will be going again.

We booked the 4 bedroom beach house for a few nights for a getaway as a family of 6 as we really wanted to try this caravan park but with a larger family we do need more space. Upon arrival, it didn’t appear terrible aside from the fact that it was advertised with “2 bathrooms”, that’s a must for us, to find 1 bathroom but 2 toilets. 6 people sharing one bathroom is awful, then to add the horrid state of the bathroom, the drizzly crap shower head, the lack of soap (the one time I don’t bring my own!) and mismatched tiles with mould in the grout really screamed poorly maintained. The disappointment grew further when it was obvious the dining table and outdoor table had not been cleaned in I don’t know how long. The dining table was sticky and gross and the outdoor table covered in weeks worth of dirt and grime. This wasn’t cheap accommodation either so pretty disappointing.

Wonderful Caravan Park.  Literally on the beach.  Beautiful beach sunrises.  Patrolled part of beach for swimming.  Dogs allowed on certain sites.  All facilities available.  Kept clean and tidy.  Many shops and cafes a stroll across the road.  Surf Club next door.  Bike paths all the way north & south along the coast.Thai Massage is really good, ask for Ping Pong.  Great park. 🙏

Only one bit of advice.  Lock up everything.  In the week I was there, an electric bike and electric scooter was stolen.
Super busy during peak times but to be expected. Nice spot and walking distance to most things. Suitable for large vans, just need to get the right spot. Some very average sites there as well.
Beautiful beach
5 nights on a caravan site... stunning location, both patrolled beach and Maroochy River... Very busy.. too be expected because the park is massive...
My family camped there from 1946 until 1997. Although obviously it's changed over the years, the river is still amazing. My Father has a plaque on the bench seat on the waterfront there.
The groundsmen is incredibly rude. It would have been a better experience if he wasn't as rude as he was. He abused and spoke down to me and my partner.

It's an absolute beautiful spot ,loved the area,the people in the park are nice & friendly, except for the one person who is the groundsman for the caravan park,he was very rude, terrible attitude & was very aggressive towards me ,I think they should get someone better to do his job ,cause he's terrible at at,I was only visiting for the day with friends,but I know I won't be coming back if he's still here 😔
Was only meant to stay 2 nights here, but extended an extra night because the location was beautiful! Will definitely be back in the future.
Awesome spot with beautiful views, close to beach.
Great spot for everyone

We have been coming to CT for over 10 years now. Booked year to year on a site that we loved. We are supposed to be coming for our annual 7 days in 2024 tomorrow and have just been informed that we have been kicked of our usual site because someone can stay longer then us. We work fulltime and can only come for a week at a time. We are very upset and disappointed and will not be coming back to CT. I know the park staff and managers don't care because we are just a number and this is now their policy, they will just replace us with someone else, its all about the money now. If you can stay for weeks v a week you will get the best sites it doesn't matter if you book the site first, which I think is extremely unfair!

We stayed in Beach House. It was sadly in need of maintenance and freshening up. Needed new Kitchen utensils, hardly any cutlery. Electric frypan was unuseable.  Big house but only one shower. Hot water was dangerously hot. Had to be careful running bath for kids.  Lovely beach and nearby cafes.

The team are Wonderful. The park is on the water.  Very clean, Amenities are clean. Lots to do and see on the sunshine coast.
We loved it and have already booked our holiday for next year.

Close to shops, nice park especially along riverfront.  Disappointed that our booked site was changed again (3rd time) and had to ask for a receipt even though I'd paid full amount on line two days before. . However, we know there is no guarantee to get our preferred site..but why is that particular site changed so frequently??  At least now you can go to reception as you enter...a little more personal than in past years.

Asked for a beachfront campsite over the phone. Was advised I had been allocated a 'better than beachfront' campsite, as it was just behind one row of campers who would block the worst of the bad weather (a stormy afternoon was forecast).

I said that sounded good, as long as I was still within the beachfront area of the campground, and would definitely not have to cross any roads to walk onto the beach. I was assured I wouldn't. I assumed this meant I would be within the grassy, treed camping area I had seen pictured, just a short walk onto the beach.

On arrival, it turned out that I had been allocated a gravel pad across the road from the beach campsites, out in the open with no tree cover, and roads running around me on 3 sides. On my other side was the backend of a motorhome, the last in a long line of motorhomes on my gravel island.
There were no other tent campers within my area. It had about as much ambience as a caravan park in Woodridge.

Did not end up staying, as reception was closed - booked alternative accomodation.

We did go for a brief walk on the beach, which was beautiful.

Would like to try and stay again, for the beach, but would pick my own site next time.

***EXTREMELY RUDE, IMMATURE AND CONDESCENDING MANAGEMENT AND STAFF***
Trying to get a 24ft van into the slither of space allocated was stressful and tight due to sites so squished together you cannot swing the nose of the car around to manouver the van in.
Management and staff in the office are incredibly rude, abusive, condescending and Immature to the point its verbal abuse. I witnessed this verbal abuse to multiple people checking in/out and to myself also for just asking if we could extend our stay.
The review comments from the park management if you care to read do not take feedback on board and are very ignorant to the issues that are going on with this park.
We won't be returning and I will be lodging a formal complaint with the council and qld ombudsman for this park to be looked into as this is extremely concerning behaviour that people and children are exposed to.
***Book with caution and don't let your children speak with or be around the staff by themselves as they are very verbally abusive***
Park is not clean, dump points overflowing onto the road with a witches hat just placed in front of the point itself. Rubbish and sewage smell along with speeding cars throughout the park. Very unsafe and lock your valuables up!

Weather your in a tent,rooftop or van this park has got you covered. Brand new facilities and camp kitchens located  evenly around and cleaned regularly, you can sit & relax or wander the nearby shops for supplies in readiness of gorgeous sunsets.....plenty of room.

This has to be one of the best caravan parks in Australia, the location is fantastic. It is so well maintained, all the facilities are spotless, very helpful park managers. Highly recommend this Caravan Park.

Booked in advance so to get a specific site by booking 6days. To stay on unpowered waterfront camp site for 5 days. Payed upfront.
When we arrived ,told we good to go in an set up on site, this was 10.30am. We arrived at site to find someone had set up on it that morning. Apparently staff at Cottontree allowed this to happen . After 1 hour or more. They told us to go to another site, which was unsuitable for us. ( As to why we booked this site as it was suitable in the first place and paid upfront) .SO WHY SHOULD YOU PAY UPFRONT WHEN CANNOT COMMIT TO THE SITE WE BOOKED? WHY LET SOMEONE ELSE TAKE THE SITE THAT MORNING WHEN IT IS BOOKED ALLREADY?
(Speaking to other campers on waterfront they also booked site they asked for and got what they asked for)
They had NO Appoligies
ALSO AT NO TIME OWNER CAN ANSWER QUESTIONS I HAVE PROVIDED,  WHY?
YOU SAY YOU APPRECIATE FEEDBACK,  BUT YOU CANNOT GIVE A GENUINE RESPONSE TO OUR QUESTION.
YOUR RESPONSE IS VERY DISRESPECTFUL TO A GENUINE QUESTIONS ASKED
Lovely caravan park, by the water.

Have been bringing our kids here since they were little from a little tent to a caravan.
In 2020 we had hired a caravan for hubbys birthday and stayed here too.
Hubby and I bought a caravan our first trip at Cotton Tree has been amazing in it.
Would highly recommend.
Service and price has been absolutely wonderful.
Beautiful spot to park up for a couple of days. Water dragons at our back door, beautiful animals

DO NOT book here in groups.
My family booked a couple sites together in close proximity as we have young children a couple months in advance.
Upon arrival we were told we cannot guarantee sites and as such our sites were moved apart and unfortunately we could not spend evenings together due to young children asleep. Put a real dampener on a holiday we had looked forward to.
Not even a sorry from reception.
This is a fantastic sport. Stay here in the caravan park about $65 a night pretty expensive expensive but really good spot.
Back again this time in a cabin, very well maintained,view of river excellent. Will come back again next year

What an amazing park. First of all big shout out to staff at marochydoore who couldn’t fit us in but suggested this gem which could. Staff at front desk were amazing, friendly and accommodating. Park is small but location perfect. Our 9 and 10yr old boys loved being so close to the beach. Amenities spotless. Lots of handy caravan accessories available at office as well! 10/10
Top spot good ppl

Great location between beach, Estuary and town.
They manage to squeeze a lot of people in but plenty of amenities to cater for the numbers.

I offered this review in person, however was treated so poorly I have to share it on here.
We had an unpowered corner site right on the beach.  The site was an amazing location, right on the beach, but the bush turkeys camp in the bush next to the site, so it smells like camping in a chicken coup.   The facilities were clean and modern.   We just spent 8 weeks travelling the whole length of Aus, and in every campground checked in with two vehicles, Cotton Tree was no exception, however  I had a problem exiting the camp ground....
We had to leave very early  in the morning, long story short, I got stuck at the gate because one code, one vehicle, which I get.  I thought I may be able to enter the code in the other lane and get out, but no.  We were never offered two codes, as we had in some other campgrounds.
There is an out of office call button on the door.  No-one answered after persistent knocking and button calling, I even call ed the security number. This was from about 5am - 6:30  I was stuck at the gate for 1.5 hours, and slightly annoyed, luckily I wasn't catching the same flight as my wife.
Now this part I have got over, as it was some weeks ago, but when I visited the area some weeks later, I called in and pointed out the  after hours button system doesn't work, with the front counter.  I was met with the most condescending behaviour from the front counter I have ever encountered.   I was spoken to like a naughty kid about the rules, and met with blame and excuses, as apposed to a 'thanks for the feedback, etc'.  All I was offereing was in person feedback about the button system.
Some upskilling needed on the front desk as to how to handle objections for sure, and how to communicate.
PS - I ended up removing the rope and bollards to exit the camp ground, so there's a tip if you need to get out, or there is an emergency.
I offered this feedback in person instead of a bad review on the first instance, but was so appalled by the response and attitude of the staff  I felt I have had to share my experience.

Wow what place to be beautiful beach
Tidy grounds and tidy toilet

Stayed here for the first time during school holidays last week. This must be one of the best campsites we have stayed at so far (if not the best). Its location to beach, shops, coffee shops pretty much everything is perfect. The staff at the desk and the staff that were maintaining the bathrooms and common areas were very friendly and the campsite was always kept in amazing condition.

Our kids always felt safe, and we felt the same for them and so many kids around they made a lot of friends too.

We will be back for sure and hoping to lock in next sept school holidays again.

very happy Camper

Colin
Ex Park stay there regularly it’s what we Queenslanders call liquid sunshine

This was the worst caravan park I have ever stayed at. Overpriced & disgusting.
Sites are uneven so it’s a struggle if you’re planning on staying in a camper trailer/caravan. Then they jam everyone in like sardines which was also a safety concern as cars parked on the corner sites block vision of kids flying through on bikes etc; it’s a tragic accident waiting to happen.
The amenities were beyond filth; that obviously says more about the larger demographic of people who camp here but during peak seasons they should be cleaned twice a day to avoid the horror I witnessed in the female toilets (everything from toilet paper all over the floors to used sanitary products hanging in the showers).
Of course the attraction to the park was its location which is a shame the caravan park was such a disgrace because otherwise it would be a great spot to return to time and time again.
Terrific family caravan park totally relaxing with friendly staff
Good location close to the river & beach & restaurants & cafes! Good clean amenities! Sites are good , some are better than others!

First time in our year of travelling Australia that we had to leave a caravan park due to not fitting into our allocated site/sites.
The ladies in the office were lovely and tried to find us a suitable site to change to even though they were fully booked, but sending us to another site that was too short for our van was a complete waste of our time and effort.
I was told “we would not have sent you to a site if we were not confident you would fit” 🤯  Our van was half a meter over into the next site 🤷‍♀️ I feel like I was treated like an idiot with a response like this.
At the time of booking I checked if the site would be big enough for our van which we were told yes, this was NOT the case.
After attempting to fit into 3 sites we were offered a refund or a site at a different Sunshine Coast park.
The location is beautiful but we are over being packed in like sardines in places like this and it seems there are no quiet periods these days.
I’m sure if u book well in advance and ask for a site larger than your van, you could have a more enjoyable experience than we did.
Location is fantastic close to everything however the caravan park is cramped. We had a corner site and we were just able to fit our car. The caravan park was busy but very quiet at night.

Great location
Sunsets amazing

We stayed for the weekend 9-12August. The park itself is very clean and maintained. Staff were friendly. Of course we had to be there when the marathon was on 😄. This meant that you could not leave the park by car due to road closures. That didn’t bother us though as the markets were on and there are plenty of shops and cafes to visit 👗👕👠☕️☕️🍹🍸. The only negative I have was the amount of unsupervised children in the bathrooms. There were kids coming in from the beach, running in and out of showers, leaving the floor saturated with water, which I felt could have been unsafe (slip and fall) for seniors wanting to use the facilities. All in all a great stay (even though we rarely do caravan parks). Highly recommend cocktails and dinner at The Boatshed Restaurant, which is conveniently located next to the park. We have booked to return in 3 weeks.

Just had a fab long weekend at Cotton Tree Holiday Park, our first time staying. We stayed on powered tent site #52 for 3 nights. It was relatively easy to reverse our camper trailer onto despite being newbies to towing and was a pleasantly large site (being on a corner I think it was a bit bigger than our neighbours along same row). Check in was easy and smooth, the reception staff friendly and helpful in telling me where to park to avoid the sunshine coast marathon road closures so we could head out that day. The amenities were excellent, the staff do a wonderful job of keeping them clean and in good working order so hats off to the cleaning crew, keep up the good work. The closest bathroom block to us was closed for repairs but that was no problem as there are plenty of facilities. We used one of the beach foreshore BBQs to cook up brekky and it too was spotless which made it so easy for us to clean for the next user. Great location, cafes and shops 2 mins walk away, some fantastic walks along the beach towards Alex and Mooloolaba, or along Cotton Tree foreshore which is also excellent and boasts many great facilities.
All in all an excellent stay spoilt only by the unbelievable heavy rain and we were trying to pack up on our morning of departure. Wish we could have stayed longer.
Thank you staff and management of the park
Our home away from home. Just a great caravan park in the best position for everything.

You have a choice of sites slab or all purpose ( san & gravel). Some sites are very tight squeeze others have good room width ways. Great location beach or river. Amenities clean fairly new but the block close to river end is not in use. Also the boom gate closest to surf not in use.

Right on the beach it is a lovely place to stay but everyone is crammed in tight. We had a corner block for our camper trailer that had lots of trees roots so was impossible to level. Facilities were very good, fairly modern and clean. Kitchen common area had the usual facilities such as kettle, cooking, fridge and microwave. Quite expensive and we had to book a minimum of 4 nights which cost is $304. We did enjoy our stay as the area was very pretty and the beach walks lovely.
Great spot right on the river front, plenty of sites, clean facilities and lovely, friendly staff

Wonderful holiday park in a great location. Right on the beach which is nice and convenient to go for a swim. So many cafes, restaurants and shops just outside the holiday park as well as a small grocery store, some liquor store. Great place for families, couples, groups, friends. Lots of great ammenities, includong laundry facilities and several toilet and shower blocks throughout the park. Wonderful place for a getaway.

We stayed at Cotton Tree on one of their powered tent sites. Kids had a blast there was so much for them to do and explore. Locations is amazing! Its also very central to other attractions the Sunshine Coast has to offer which we made the most of. Cant wait to visit again next year!

This caravan park is possibly the best park we have ever stayed at. We just have a rooftop tent but the spaces were massive and very well laid out. The staff were very friendly and helpful and helped us with suggestions on orientation of our vehicle and our friends large caravan.

The amenities are the cleanest I have ever come across and the water pressure in the showers is fantastic. The beach is only a small walk (depending on where you stay) and it backs straight onto it so it's great for kids or if you like fishing/swimming ECT.

Location wise it's close to alot of cafes and shops so easily accessible on a morning coffee run. Definitely can't fault anything here and we will be back soon hopefully for a longer stay.
great amenities. friendly staff. clean sites. very big. lots of people. short walk to the beach. close to shops

Excellent location, close to beach and shops, cafes etc. Facilities nice and clean. Got the worst site in the park under a tree no television reception for the whole week. Went to reception were told "it can be a bit iffy".  Called into Jaycar to see if they could help, straight away asked if we were at Cotton Tree Holiday Park 🙄. Unfortunately nothing can help.
Booked 12 days with a day b4 booking got us in, great help over the phone booking, beautiful sunsets, clean amenities, kind staff

I love this place. It’s fantastic for families who love to get out and about.
The entire park is well looked after.
The cleaning crews must be subcontractors as I saw them come in and clean the amenities, and I must say, they do a wonderful job.
The staff are incredibly helpful and very welcoming and I love that they get in food trucks also.

The ONLY problem I have noticed is that due to the narrow roads the the park, it can be a very big challenge to get larger vans into the sites, but ones you get that part out of the way, it’s certainly all relax and unwind.

Big fan

Had a wonderful week camping all the  way from Bundaberg to Airlie Beach and back again.  We rang ahead to cotton tree holiday Park to book for our rooftop tent. Just 1 night. $65 😳 that's a lot for a powered site !! We arrived at 2.30pm plenty of time to set up.  On arrival, it looked VERY busy, over 300 sites here!! The 1st thing we were told was that the  amenities block closest to us ..was closed. The 1st site they gave us was right by the mains sewage terminal, uneven, and it would have used all of our 15mt power lead to have reached the power point. I went to ask for another one. The second one had a large tree in the parking space,  as pictured,  not suitable as we have a rooftop tent..Having looked all around us , none of the sites are level, to be honest we have driven for the last 5.5 hrs and Really just want to set up and settle down. We decided to leave. I went to the reception and said the last site WASN'T suitable and we wanted to leave. I was told that there were plenty of sites if we just looked around.  I asked for a refund.  I was told that there was a 7 day refund policy... REALLY !!! we hadn't booked 7 days ago .. I had rung that morning ?? I said that we were not happy and that we would be leaving.  NO REFUND..   so, in conclusion . If you are happy to stay on a site that is vast, with sites close together that you can hear what is going on with your NEAREST neighbour!! Then I'm sure you will be fine. However, having used many other caravan/holiday parks at reasonable prices,  I would certainly not recommend Cotton tree holiday Park.  Unfortunate,  because we have friends who love the place , but can't see  why??
Nice area beautiful beach close to surf club
I arrive lateand they still accomodate me to check in. The cleanest caravan park toilet i ever been. Thank you
We stayed in a van for 3 days. The view was amazing and just next to the beach.

Caravan park.
Calm inlet on one side of the park, surf  patrolled beachon the other, perfect!
Local cafe, boutiques and weekend markets on the door step.
What's not to like 👌

Campsite is great but you are crammed in like sardines. We went in May when it was quiet, would be aweful in summer!
Facilities are awesome, clean today and well maintained. Excellent access to river and beach, and the nearby shops and restaurants.
Such a ripper of a camp ground. Great for kids. Beach is gorgeous.  Recommend!!!!!
One of the very best spots in Australia!

I live on the Sunshine Coast and my girlfriend came up with her camper and we stayed here for a few nights. It was close to the water and so lovely to stay near the beach, as though you were on holidays. It was so peaceful at dawn and dusk and we did the sun and the swim of course.

We have been camping at this campground for a very long time. We have had good times and bad times. We stop going for a while because the camp rangers that we're there were lazy and didn't seem to care too much. RECENTLY we stayed there again and discovered they had new  camp rangers and WOW what a difference. We had a great time with very extremely clean facilities and well maintained grounds. We the staff very friendly and very helpful. There was a very bad situation that did occur while we were there involving a father & son get together from a school in Brisbane.  This group of over 300 were totally disrespectful to all other camper in the park. They drank from 4pm  till 2am in the morning, being extremely vocal and rude. One father thought it was appropriate to walk outside and pee in front of me and little children going past on bikes and swing it around. This incident obviously upset many many campers whose weekend was ruined by no sleep and unruly children not being supervised by their fathers as they were too busy getting pissed. But I will say that there were about 25% of the dads who didn't drink and did spend time with their boys. NOW as unfortunate as this all was it was certainly handled by the staff of the park in a very respectful way to us & other campers and even to the disrespectful crowd, even though they didn't get respect back they stayed very calm and respectful themselves. My understanding is this group are NOT being allowed to return. My family and myself praise the Cotton Tree team especially Jason as he dealt with them the most for handling this situation with the respect for all the way that they did. They are new in taking over the park and not dealt with group before ( as they had camp there before) but we feel they handle the situation appropriately and respectfully to all concerned. Keep up the good work guys the place is starting to look amazing and we look forward to coming back. And I ask everyone including the people who stayed there on the Labor Day long weekend and had a bad time to try again and give these guys a chance they are making a difference but it won't happen over night but it has well and truly started. Anyway great job guys thankyou for everything you did in assisting us with the situation and just for giving us a NICE CLEAN, BEAUTIFUL, FRIENDLY 3 week holiday.

We love camping here as locals but it’s really getting worse not better. The sites are so muddy and not grassy at all. And they are renovating facilities and didn’t notify us the toilets we were located near were closed. Not ideal with 3 small children.
Quiet, close to shops and great beach for my sup

One of my favourite holiday parks on the Sunshine Coast! I loved the convenient location, the clean rooms and the friendly staff. I will definitely be back again and will be recommending to all my friends and family :)
I have never see the park so well maintained, the kids had a ball and we will be back for sure! What an amazing experience! Staff were awesome!
Lovely place, can get noisy and extra busy during school holidays.  Best to be away from the back fence. The little shops are so busy and the noise starts around five.

We love this place!! We’ve been coming here for years, but over the past 12 months we’ve seen such an amazing change! The staff are lovely and we’ve never seen the grounds so beautiful. It’s so well maintained, so close to shops and restaurants. This place is a must for anyone for a family holiday or just a romantic weekend getaway with an incredible sunset. We went over the school holidays and the park had even gone to the extent to putting on live music and food trucks!

The location is great on the river but the sites are just too small for what you pay.  Definitely have profit in mind rather than relaxation- you can go to quality Big4 with much more for the price.  I watched countless people spend substantial time trying fit into their spots and cars had to be shuffled many times. My husband also mentioned the male amenities were awful while we were there - vomit left on the floor for multiple days, toilets overflowing. I appreciate that it is expected guests will clean up after themselves but stuff like that should be cleaned as it was plain unhygienic for other guests. Location is good in high tide but obviously average in low tide, Staff are very rude surprisingly since the location has not much to offer other then the Maroochy River. We then sent an email with no response until I had a staff member ring morning of booking being very passive aggressive. Very sad that their attitude will loose this location many returning visits.

We’ve stayed at Cotton tree for Easter the past few years and have never been disappointed. Everytime you interact with a staff member it’s always positive.
Very helpful, friendly and an amazing view at the waterfront!

We had booked our waterfront site same time last year for this time,manager guaranteed the site at booking! We were so looking fwd to our break! When arrived, they had us on a site long way from where we were promised!! UPSET WE WERE!!
I could go on & on!! BBQs not clean! Management had long noses 🤥 of excuses!
A huge Damper on our trip! & I’m not one in the last few years to Whinge!

Our first trip out in our motor home. Great location. Space for parking is adequate
Suggestions- If you call to book don't leave a message, I called on a Thursday & they got back to me on Saturday. Thankfully my wife was able to secure a site. Overall this is a good experience.

Apparently “changing the booking name” is part of their “Cancellation policy”.

Someone will still use the spot, just under a different name. So technically it’s not cancelling the booking, just changing the booking name. Pathetic.
Fabulous caravan park at the mouth of the Maroochy river.  Great views out to see and across the river

Pro:
* great spot so near to the beach.
*Cleaners do an awesome job keeping the amenities spotless! - I pity them after seeing how some campers treated the amenities
* groundskeepers/gardeners keep the park looking great!
Cons:
* disabled bathroom has been unusable since at least October last year- keypad doesn’t work-I’m an ambulatory wheelchair user and we specifically asked to be near a disabled bathroom as I can’t shower on my own and had to walk to another bathroom as where we were I couldn’t get my wheelchair through the dirt/mud and ended up having to drive to the nearest bathroom and park on someone’s site.
* nearest amenities had no hot water for several days even though we called to let them know nothing was done about it

Although this is a good spot near the beach, shops etc, for the price I expected that I’d be able to use the amenities that were close to where they put us, especially as they were told that I’m disabled and needed to be near a disabled bathroom.
Not really a good place for anyone with a disability unless they put you near a bathroom that works.

Fantastic caravan park right on the water. Large spots for all size camping.

No pets allowed
Great place to stay, right on the beach. Friendly and helpful staff. Great amenities
Amazing

Tick, tick, tick. Second time we have stayed here. Staff friendly and courteous. Facilities clean and presented well. A gentle stroll to the beach and multiple shops across the road.

Thanks Cotton Tree, we will be back!

Soooo clean and tidy and great size plots for the caravan, outdoor area and your car. The amenities are spotless 😃
Best of both worlds, access to the Maroochydore river and the ocean at your doorstep. Heaps of resturants, bars, cafes and shops in walking distance. Perfect location, perfect holiday destination  ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

You can't reserve a site, even after paying. The staff have no idea, and are happy to put you in a site that is too small. I'm not new to reversing into small areas,  but it literally took us 20 min,  and we're about to give up.

That said,  they were happy to place a small campervan onto a large and easy access site straight after us??!!

Apparently this is not a unique situation, and spoke with other campers with a slide out van that couldn't extend there van due to a tree on the site.

Honestly,  look else where,  so you don't start your holiday with a sour taste.  We were part of a large group,  and none of our group will be coming back, as we have all had issues since arriving.

Fantastic location, but unfortunately, the customer service and booking system for caravan sites is so backward. We were promised a site next to our friends and family which was suitable for our size caravan as we have a large setup. Upon arrival, we were told we couldn't reserve sites and were moved to a site that was FAR too small for our caravan.  It took at least twenty minutes to squeeze our van onto this site and was a  highly stressful situation. As we were trying to navigate onto our site we saw a small camper van setting up on the site we were promised which could have easily fit anywhere. There were plenty of suitable sites vacant however they chose to squeeze us onto a small site.
On top of that, I had been calling the office all day to extend our stay one more night with no one answering.
When we got to the park I went into the office and asked to book one more night. They were extremely unhelpful and said we would have to relocate after one night to a smaller site again because someone had reserved the site we are currently on
That makes zero sense as we didn't get our promised site as they told us they don't allow reservations for particular sites and that is why we were moved to begin with.
Talking to other caravaners, many shared very similar experiences as us. It's disappointing as it's a beautiful spot but due to poor organisation, and lack of customer care, we won't be returning.
Great spot 👍
The location is good but the camp site is  way too small. It is too crowded. For the price they should offer a little bit more space.

Cotton Tree forever ruined for us.

Close to the river and the waves. Great location. The camp sites are quite small and take some manoeuvring to get in and out, but overall, it was great getaway.

Love this spot.

Really heartbroken over the treatment my mum and dad have received from Nicole after nearly 25 years of bookings. My father has brain cancer and I asked Nicole if we could change our Christmas site from a 6 week booking to a 3 week booking as my dad was having treatment during this time. Nicole advised me that it's is a 6 week site and a 6 week site only. I was then told that "it wasn't her problem", and "I could take the site for 6 weeks or leave it". She then said "I don't want your money, I have people ringing for a 6 week site everyday". She also said to not bother with the council as they won't do anything to help me. She then hung up on me.  I could not understand why we could not reduce our booking for the future. How this woman could not help out a man who is terminally ill and has been a part of the cotton tree caravan park community for 25 years is beyond me. Luckily I recorded the phone conversation and I have taken our story to all major media outlets and also a local Sunshine coast MP. I am still so upset with how I was treated by Nicole as she showed me no empathy at all over this devastating  situation.

We asked to br put as close to the beach as possible but were placed at site 158 amongst the permanent homes. Not impressed... it took us 15 mins to walk to the beach with 2 little kids.
3rd night 2 young kids were riding their electric bikes and smashed right into our 2 year old. We tried calling managers at 7pm phones off and no way to contact them... I guess all they worry about it $$$$ and really not being of service.
